title: How to factor 2048 bit RSA integers with less than a million noisy qubits
abstract: Argues a fault-tolerant quantum computer with <1M physical qubits can factor RSA-2048 in <1 week — a ~20x qubit reduction over Gidney-Ekera 2019 (~20M qubits). Gains come from approximate residue arithmetic (Chevignard-Fouque-Schrottenloher 2024), yoked surface codes for idle logical qubits (Gidney-Newman-Brooks-Jones 2023), and magic-state cultivation reducing distillation footprint (Gidney-Shutty-Jones 2024).
relevance_note: RSA not ECC, but the enabling techniques transfer directly: approximate/residue arithmetic to shrink registers, yoked surface codes for cheap idle storage, and magic-state cultivation to slash distillation overhead. These are the same space- and distillation-aware levers used in the 2026 ECDLP estimates; useful for cost-modeling the ECDSA-Fail circuit.

title: Space-Efficient Quantum Algorithm for Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithms with Resource Estimation
abstract: Optimized quantum circuit for ECDLP minimizing logical qubits. Core contribution is a space-efficient reversible modular inversion algorithm using length registers and location-controlled arithmetic to compactly store intermediate variables of the extended-Euclid/Kaliski inversion.
relevance_note: QUBIT-MINIMIZER via modular inversion. Modular inversion circuit: 3n + 4*floor(log2 n) + O(1) qubits, 204 n^2 log2(n) + O(n^2) Toffoli. Full ECDLP: 5n + 4*floor(log2 n) + O(1) qubits, O(n^3) Toffoli. Concrete n=256: 1333 logical qubits (vs 2124 in Häner et al.). The compact-inversion register layout is a directly portable space-saving technique.

title: Optimized Point Addition Circuits for Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithms
abstract: Presents fully-disclosed optimized point-addition quantum logical circuits over prime fields, achieving results comparable to the (undisclosed) Babbush et al. / Google Quantum AI circuits. Gives explicit gate counts for secp256k1 and a generic prime-field variant valid for any prime field.
relevance_note: DIRECT SOTA-RELEVANT, 2026. Independently rediscovers the Google ECDLP point-add optimization (which Google released only via a zero-knowledge proof). For secp256k1: ~1.5% MORE qubits but 6.5-10% FEWER Toffoli gates than Babbush et al. Provides a closed-form generic prime-field point-addition gate-count formula — directly usable as a circuit template for the ECDSA-Fail benchmark.

title: Reducing T-count with the ZX-calculus
abstract: Represents a Clifford+T circuit as a ZX-diagram, simplifies it with the graph-theoretic ZX rewrite rules, and introduces 'phase teleportation' to let non-Clifford phases combine and cancel non-locally without changing circuit structure. Matches or beats prior T-count reducers on most benchmarks, with up to ~50% reduction in the ancilla-free case.
relevance_note: Phase teleportation / phase-gadget fusion: merges and cancels T-phases scattered across the circuit, cutting T-count up to ~50% with no extra qubits. Core technique behind PyZX. Directly applicable to reducing T in the modular-arithmetic blocks of ECC point addition.

title: Reversible Pebbling Game for Quantum Memory Management
abstract: Quantum memory management is becoming a pressing problem. The order in which intermediate results are computed and uncomputed -- governed by the reversible pebble game -- greatly affects both the number of qubits used and the gate count. Formulates pebbling as a SAT/QBF problem to find pebbling strategies that minimize qubits subject to a circuit-size budget.
relevance_note: Reversible pebbling: solver-driven scheduling of compute/uncompute to minimize ancilla qubits at the cost of recomputation. Directly applicable to choosing the uncompute order of intermediate registers in modular-arithmetic / EC point-addition pipelines.

title: Choosing Coordinate Forms for Solving ECDLP Using Shor's Algorithm
abstract: Investigates whether projective coordinates beat affine for Shor-ECDLP. Builds reversible out-of-place point-addition circuits in projective coordinates for Weierstrass and Edwards curves, then uses Bennett's trick to make them in-place, and analyzes the quantum resources. Concludes projective coordinates do NOT give the same advantage as affine because projective representations are non-unique without modular division.
relevance_note: AFFINE-vs-PROJECTIVE TRADE-OFF (cautionary). Key finding: naive projective coordinates do not help because non-uniqueness of (X:Y:Z) reintroduces modular division to canonicalize, defeating the point of avoiding inversion. Withdrawn, but its negative result is exactly what Chevignard-Fouque-Schrottenloher 2026 sidestep with the Legendre-symbol hash — important context for any projective-coordinate route in the benchmark.

title: Validation of Quantum Elliptic Curve Point Addition Circuits
abstract: Identifies and corrects inconsistencies in state-of-the-art quantum point-addition circuits, ensuring ancilla qubits are restored to their original states (to avoid decoherence/garbage). Surfaces four specific issues and provides fixes without increasing gate cost.
relevance_note: CORRECTNESS / ANCILLA-CLEANUP. Catalogs four concrete bugs in published point-add circuits where ancillas were not properly uncomputed — directly relevant to the ECDSA-Fail validity (cls/pha/anc) checks. Fixes cost zero extra gates, so adopting them is a free correctness win when reusing a literature point-add design.

title: A Classical-Quantum Adder with Constant Workspace and Linear Gates
abstract: Resolves the open question of whether classical-quantum adders (adding a classically-known constant into a quantum register) can match the O(n)-gate, O(1)-ancilla complexity of Cuccaro's quantum-quantum adder. Gives an adder using 3 clean ancillae and 4n±O(1) Toffolis, and a variant with 3n±O(1) Toffolis using 2 clean + n-2 dirty ancillae. Controlled versions require no extra workspace or Toffolis.
relevance_note: CONSTANT-ADDITION: modular-arithmetic circuits constantly add the classically-known modulus/curve constants; this gives a 3n-4n Toffoli, O(1)-clean-ancilla in-place constant adder, and crucially a *free* control (no extra Toffolis/space when conditioning on a control qubit). Directly cuts Toffoli + qubit cost of the modular reductions inside secp256k1 point-add.

title: Verifiable Quantum Advantage via Optimized DQI Circuits
abstract: Applies Decoded Quantum Interferometry to the Optimal Polynomial Intersection problem (dual code = Reed-Solomon), giving the first candidate for verifiable quantum advantage with optimal asymptotic speedup: classical hardness O(2^N) vs only Õ(N) quantum gates. The bottleneck reversible Reed-Solomon decoder needs an efficient Extended Euclidean Algorithm; introduces a new 'Dialog' representation for implicit Bézout-coefficient access and optimized in-place architectures that cut the leading-order cost.
relevance_note: DIALOG / EEA-SPLIT: the 'Dialog' implicit-Bezout representation and in-place Extended-Euclidean-Algorithm circuit. Modular inversion in affine EC point-addition is exactly an EEA/GCD computation — these in-place EEA constructions and the Dialog trick (avoid materializing both Bezout coefficients) are a direct Toffoli+qubit lever for the field-inversion step of secp256k1 affine point-add.

title: Quantum Circuit Optimization with AlphaTensor (AlphaTensor-Quantum)
abstract: Casts T-count minimization as symmetric tensor (rank) decomposition and solves it with a deep-RL agent (extension of AlphaTensor), incorporating gadget-based domain knowledge. Outperforms prior T-count optimizers on arithmetic benchmarks and rediscovers Karatsuba-like finite-field multiplication.
relevance_note: RL + tensor-rank decomposition for T-count: finds gadgetized low-T circuits, beating heuristic optimizers on Shor-relevant arithmetic (finite-field multiplication). Directly targets the kind of modular-multiplication blocks in ECC point addition.

title: Trade-offs between classical and quantum space using spooky pebbling
abstract: Applies the spooky pebble game to general circuits (not just lines) for the first time. Proves an upper bound on quantum space in the spooky pebble game, proves solving the game is PSPACE-complete, and presents a SAT-solver-plus-heuristic solver that computes optimal classical-space / quantum-space / time trade-offs empirically.
relevance_note: A practical solver that schedules measurement-based uncomputation (spooks) on arbitrary DAGs to trade classical bits for qubits. Directly usable to find qubit-minimal uncompute orderings for EC point-addition dependency graphs.

title: More on Fast Constant-Time GCD Computation and Modular Inversion (safegcd2) + jumpdivsteps / secp256k1 implementation notes
authors: Daniel J. Bernstein, Bo-Yin Yang (safegcd2); Peter Dettman & Pieter Wuille (secp256k1 batching); sipa/gmaxwell safegcd-bounds
venue: Workshop 2024 + Bitcoin Core secp256k1 docs + safegcd-bounds repos
abstract: Refines divstep bounds and presents the batched 'jumpdivsteps' technique: take the low k bits of f and g, run k divsteps on just those bottom bits while recording which branches were taken, accumulate a single (k+2)-bit 2x2 transition matrix, then apply that ONE matrix to the full-width f,g. secp256k1 does 62 divsteps per batch, updating full operands only ~12 times for 256-bit. safegcd-bounds (sipa/gmaxwell) proves 724 divsteps suffice (vs 741 claimed).
relevance_note: jumpdivsteps is the key lever for reversible cost: instead of n full-width controlled updates (each O(n) Toffoli), batch T divsteps into one small 2x2 matrix computed on k bottom bits (cheap, narrow), then do ~n/T full-width matrix-mults. This decouples the expensive wide arithmetic count from the iteration count, and the small transition matrix is the only per-batch state to record reversibly. Directly relevant to shrinking the (u,v,r,s)/Toffoli budget in EC point addition.

title: The Jacobi Factoring Circuit: Quantum Factoring with Near-Linear Gates and Sublinear Space and Depth
authors: Gregory D. Kahanamoku-Meyer, Seyoon Ragavan, Vinod Vaikuntanathan, Katherine Van Kirk (et al.)
abstract: Quantum factoring circuit with near-linear gate count and sublinear space/depth, built around a reversible Jacobi-symbol computation. The Jacobi symbol is evaluated via a binary-GCD-style reciprocity recursion (the same shift/swap/reciprocity-flip structure as Stein's binary GCD), implemented as a constant-time reversible circuit.
relevance_note: Demonstrates a fully reversible binary-GCD-flavored circuit in the wild: the Jacobi-symbol recursion IS a binary GCD where you additionally track the symbol sign through quadratic-reciprocity flips. The accumulator/sign-tracking and shift-swap layout are directly transferable to a reversible Kaliski/divstep inverse, and tie to Schrottenloher's Legendre-symbol-to-one-bit compression. Strong evidence that GCD/Jacobi reciprocity circuits can hit near-linear gates and sublinear space.

title: Improved quantum circuits for elliptic curve discrete logarithms
abstract: Improved quantum circuits for EC scalar multiplication in Shor's algorithm: optimized low-level reversible integer and modular arithmetic, windowed scalar multiplication, an improved (Montgomery-style) modular inversion, and an affine Weierstrass point-addition circuit with reduced depth and T-count. Q# implementation with resource estimates and explicit cost trade-offs.
relevance_note: KEY OPTIMIZATION PAPER. Windowed point addition (lookup-table comb) cuts the number of point-addition steps and Toffoli count substantially vs double-and-add. Reports ~2124 logical qubits for n=256 (the figure later beaten by Luo/Chevignard/Schrottenloher). Introduces the qubit/Toffoli/T-depth trade-off knobs (window size, in-place vs out-of-place division) that downstream space-optimized designs tune.

title: Quantum resource estimates for computing elliptic curve discrete logarithms
abstract: Precise quantum resource estimates for Shor's elliptic-curve discrete-log algorithm over prime fields. Simulating Toffoli networks for controlled EC point addition (in LIQUi|>), shows ECDLP over an n-bit prime field needs at most 9n+2*ceil(log2 n)+10 qubits and at most 448 n^3 log2(n) + 4090 n^3 Toffoli gates.
relevance_note: The canonical ECDLP/ECC circuit-resource paper and the direct ancestor of the ecdsa.fail point-addition benchmark. Specifies the full reversible modular-arithmetic stack (modular add/sub, Montgomery multiply, modular inverse via extended-GCD) and the 9n-ish qubit / Toffoli accounting that later work shaves.

title: How to compute a 256-bit elliptic curve private key with only 50 million Toffoli gates
abstract: Resource estimate for Shor-ECDLP as a case study for the active-volume (silicon-photonics-inspired) fault-tolerant architecture. Non-local connections cut cost-per-key by 300-700x; architecture-independent algorithmic modifications reduce Toffoli count per key by up to 5x by reusing stored state across multiple keys and spreading the modular-division cost over parallel algorithm instances.
relevance_note: DISTILLATION-AWARE TOFFOLI MINIMIZER. ~50M Toffoli for one 256-bit key (down from the ~5x-larger naive count) via state reuse and amortizing modular division across parallel instances. At 10% threshold / 10-us cycle: one key every ~10 min on 6000 modules x 1152 physical qubits each. Establishes that algorithmic Toffoli reduction (not just hardware) drives cost; the 'amortize the inversion' idea is a portable lever.

title: Short-Iteration / Compact and Efficient Constant-Time GCD and Modular Inversion with Short-Iteration
abstract: Proposes short-iteration constant-time GCD (CT-GCD) and constant-time modular inversion algorithms that reduce the number of divstep/Kaliski iterations versus Bos, Bernstein-Yang, and the improved BY, with experimental speedups. Tightens the worst-case iteration bound that constant-time padding must reach.
relevance_note: Iteration count is the loop-unroll depth of any reversible inverse circuit, and Toffoli cost scales linearly with it. Lowering the proven worst-case bound (e.g. fewer than 2n, or fewer than the 741/724 safegcd bound for 256-bit) directly cuts the number of unrolled rounds — and thus branch-bit ancillas — in a reversible Kaliski/divstep circuit. Useful as the 'how few rounds can we get away with' reference.

title: New circuits and an open source decoder for the color code
abstract: Improved stabilizer-measurement circuits and an open-source decoder for the color code, which supports more efficient logical operations than the surface code at the cost of harder syndrome extraction/decoding.
relevance_note: Alternative FT substrate (color code) with cheaper logical gates; potential future lever for reducing the spacetime cost of Clifford-heavy or transversal portions of an arithmetic circuit. Lower direct relevance to Toffoli/qubit count.

title: Factoring with n+2 clean qubits and n-1 dirty qubits
abstract: Presents reversible circuits for modular multiplication, modular exponentiation, and Shor's factoring that use n+2 clean qubits plus n-1 borrowed dirty qubits (qubits in an unknown state borrowed from elsewhere and returned untouched). Uses dirty-ancilla constant adders and controlled modular arithmetic.
relevance_note: Borrowed/dirty-ancilla construction: hosts scratch on n-1 qubits owned by other registers, restoring them exactly, so net clean-qubit footprint is only n+2. Register-borrowing instead of allocation -- a direct space lever for arithmetic blocks.

title: Unqomp: Synthesizing Uncomputation in Quantum Circuits
abstract: First procedure to automatically synthesize uncomputation in a given quantum circuit, freeing temporary ancilla values to |0> so they can be safely reused. Integrates into Qiskit-style languages, letting programmers allocate temporaries like in classical computation, with Unqomp guaranteeing correct, efficient uncomputation.
relevance_note: Automatic uncomputation: builds a qubit-dependency graph and inserts uncompute gates to free ancilla as early as safely possible, reducing peak qubit count without manual scheduling. Directly relevant to garbage-free ancilla reuse in arithmetic circuits.

title: Reinforcement Learning Based Quantum Circuit Optimization via ZX-Calculus
abstract: Trains an RL agent to choose and order ZX-calculus rewrite rules (local complementation, pivoting, phase gadgetization) to minimize gate/T-count, outperforming fixed-heuristic ZX simplification routines such as PyZX's full_reduce on benchmark circuits.
relevance_note: RL-guided ZX rewriting: learns the rewrite-ordering policy that fixed PyZX heuristics miss, squeezing extra 2-qubit-gate and T-count reduction out of the ZX representation. Applicable on top of phase teleportation.

title: Reducing the Number of Qubits in Quantum Discrete Logarithms on Elliptic Curves
abstract: Space-optimized Shor-ECDLP achieving space complexity 3.12n for an n-bit curve. Key trick: output compression via a single-bit hash function based on the Legendre symbol — computing the Legendre symbol of projective coordinates instead of doing explicit modular inversion, which removes the most expensive (qubit-hungry) subroutine from the critical register.
relevance_note: STRONGEST QUBIT-MINIMIZER, 2026. 256-bit ECDLP in 1098 logical qubits (down from 2124 of Häner et al.) — ~3.12n space. The Legendre-symbol output compression eliminates explicit modular inversion in the projective representation; major lever for qubit-count-limited benchmarks like ECDSA-Fail. EUROCRYPT 2026.

title: Securing Elliptic Curve Cryptocurrencies against Quantum Vulnerabilities: Resource Estimates and Mitigations
abstract: New resource estimates for breaking 256-bit ECDLP over secp256k1. Shor's algorithm runs with either <=1200 logical qubits and <=90M Toffoli, or <=1450 logical qubits and <=70M Toffoli. On superconducting hardware (1e-3 error, planar connectivity) executes in minutes with <500k physical qubits. Logical point-addition circuits are NOT disclosed; validated via a zero-knowledge proof for responsible disclosure.
relevance_note: CURRENT GOOGLE SOTA, 2026. The ~20x reduction over Litinski's 2023 physical-qubit figure that set the modern bar. Two operating points: (1200 qubits, 90M Toffoli) and (1450 qubits, 70M Toffoli) — the qubit/Toffoli Pareto frontier. Circuits withheld but Schrottenloher 2026 (2606.02235) reconstructs comparable ones. The benchmark target to beat.

title: Windowed quantum arithmetic
abstract: A quantum analogue of classical windowing: small table lookups (QROM) let control qubits be processed in groups of w instead of one at a time. Presents windowed modular exponentiation with nested windowed modular multiplications, achieving lower Toffoli counts than prior work from tens to thousands of qubits.
relevance_note: WINDOWED ARITHMETIC: replace w per-bit controlled multiply-adds with a single QROM table lookup keyed on a w-bit window, then one addition. Trades 2^w-ish lookup Toffolis for a factor-~w reduction in multiply-add Toffolis. The dominant Toffoli-reduction lever for the scalar-multiplication / modular-multiply core of EC point-addition; pick w to balance lookup vs add cost.

title: Constant time modular inversion
abstract: Modifies Kaliski's Montgomery-inverse algorithm so the (u,v,r,s) loop runs in constant time (fixed iteration count 2n, branchless conditional updates) to defeat simple power analysis, computing both classical and Montgomery inverse with identical control flow regardless of input.
relevance_note: Constant-time = input-independent control flow = exactly the property a reversible quantum circuit REQUIRES (the gate sequence cannot depend on superposed data). Bos's branchless reformulation of Kaliski (fixed 2n iterations, masked conditional swaps/adds) is the direct template for how to lay out Kaliski reversibly: pad to worst-case iteration count, replace each data-dependent branch with a controlled (Toffoli-masked) update gated on a recorded branch qubit.

title: Rise of conditionally clean ancillae for efficient quantum circuit constructions
abstract: Introduces 'conditionally clean' ancillae, a resource bridging clean and dirty ancillae: like dirty ancillae they start/end in an unknown state and can be borrowed from existing system qubits (no allocation overhead), but like clean ancillae they can be treated as |0> within specific computations, avoiding toggle-detection overhead. Introduces 'Laddered Toggle Detection' to avoid exponential overhead, and shows one part of a circuit can borrow dirty bits from another part of itself.
relevance_note: Conditionally-clean ancilla: borrow bits from existing registers and use them as if clean, eliminating both allocation cost and the usual dirty-ancilla toggle-detection blowup. Enables linear/log-depth multi-controlled gates with zero extra qubits -- a fresh space lever for control-heavy EC circuits.

title: Magic state cultivation: growing T states as cheap as CNOT gates
abstract: Introduces 'magic state cultivation', which grows a single T state inside a surface-code patch using roughly the same number of physical gates as a lattice-surgery CNOT of equivalent reliability. Reaches logical error ~2e-9 at p~1e-3 (and ~4e-11 at p~5e-4) using an order of magnitude fewer qubit-rounds than prior distillation. Suggests further distillation may never be needed in practice.
relevance_note: Drastically lowers the per-T spacetime cost (qubit-rounds per magic state) — the conversion factor from Toffoli/T count to surface-code volume. By making a T state ~as cheap as a CNOT, it shifts the FT cost balance away from distillation factories toward logical-qubit storage and routing, reinforcing that idle qubit x time and Toffoli-count are both first-order terms in the objective.

title: Adaptive Clifford+T Decomposition of Large Toffoli Gates with One Clean Ancilla
abstract: Gives measurement-adaptive Clifford+T constructions for multi-controlled Toffoli (MCX) gates using only one clean ancilla, lowering T-count and T-depth versus standard ancilla-heavy decompositions by exploiting mid-circuit measurement and feedforward.
relevance_note: Measurement-adaptive multi-controlled Toffoli with a single clean ancilla: reduces both T-count and ancilla for large MCX gates - relevant when ECC circuits need wide controlled operations under tight qubit budgets.

title: Active volume: An architecture for efficient fault-tolerant quantum computers with limited non-local connections
abstract: Introduces an architecture where the cost of a computation is the number of qubits times the number of non-Clifford gates, replaced by a per-operation 'active volume'. With O(log N) non-local connections per logical qubit, cost scales with logical operations rather than qubit count; for thousands of logical qubits active volume can be orders of magnitude below circuit volume, and the machine runs with spacetime cost ~2x the active volume.
relevance_note: Active-volume reframes the space-time tradeoff: idle qubits cost nothing, so the metric becomes summed per-operation volume. Changes which uncomputation/ancilla strategies are optimal -- favoring constructions that minimize active (non-Clifford) volume over raw qubit count.

title: Reusability Report: Optimizing T-count in General Quantum Circuits with AlphaTensor-Quantum
abstract: Independent reusability evaluation of AlphaTensor-Quantum: reproduces and probes the deep-RL T-count optimizer on general (non-arithmetic) circuits, characterizing where the gadget-based tensor-decomposition approach helps and where it underperforms classical ZX/phase-polynomial methods.
relevance_note: Critical follow-up on AlphaTensor-Quantum: clarifies the practical T-count gains/limits of the RL approach vs ZX and T-par on general circuits, useful for deciding whether RL is worth deploying on ECC arithmetic.

title: Tight Bounds on the Spooky Pebble Game: Recycling Qubits with Measurements
abstract: The reversible pebble game models qubit reuse in quantum algorithms but cannot exploit the power of irreversible intermediate measurements. The spooky pebble game models interleaved measurements and adaptive phase corrections, reducing qubit count beyond reversible approaches. Proves asymptotically tight time-qubit trade-offs for the spooky pebble game on a line for any pebble bound, giving a tight time-qubit tradeoff for simulating arbitrary classical sequential computation.
relevance_note: Tight space-time bounds for measurement-based uncomputation on a line: quantifies exactly how many qubits measurement+phase-fixup saves vs reversible pebbling for a given recomputation budget. The theoretical backbone for spooky-pebbling-based qubit recycling.

title: Parallel Spooky Pebbling Makes Regev Factoring More Practical
abstract: Develops parallel spooky pebbling strategies and applies them to reduce the qubit/space cost of Regev's factoring algorithm, exploiting measurement-based uncomputation across parallel branches to cut quantum space while keeping depth manageable.
relevance_note: Extends measurement-based uncomputation to the parallel setting, letting several recomputation branches reclaim qubits concurrently via spooks -- a depth-aware space lever relevant when uncompute scheduling must respect parallelism in EC/Shor pipelines.

title: Reducing the Number of Qubits in Quantum Computations (Scalable Memory Recycling for Large Quantum Programs)
abstract: Presents a scalable approach to recycle qubit memory across large quantum programs by automatically scheduling uncomputation and reuse of ancilla registers, extending the Unqomp line of work to handle realistic, modular programs while minimizing peak qubit usage.
relevance_note: Scales automatic uncomputation/memory-recycling to whole programs: schedules when freed registers can host later temporaries, lowering peak qubit count. Useful for register sharing across the many sub-blocks of an EC point-addition circuit.

title: Quantum Catalytic Space
abstract: Defines catalytic computing in the quantum setting and gives initial results. Shows that reusing borrowed full ('catalytic') memory that must be returned to its original state is a powerful computational resource, enabling subroutines with little or no net space overhead.
relevance_note: Catalytic computation theory: borrow a full, arbitrarily-initialized register, use it as workspace, and restore it exactly -- net-zero ancilla footprint. Generalizes dirty-ancilla borrowing and motivates register-sharing tricks that host garbage in already-occupied qubits.

title: On Exact Space-Depth Trade-Offs in Multi-Controlled Toffoli Decomposition
abstract: Studies exact trade-offs between ancilla count and circuit depth when decomposing multi-controlled Toffoli gates, integrating Selinger/Jones/Soeken measurement-based-update designs (one ancilla, T-count 4, T-depth 1) and ancilla-free logical-AND variants. Characterizes when replacing Toffolis with ANDs plus measurement-based uncomputation reduces depth at the price of ancilla, or vice versa.
relevance_note: Maps the exact ancilla-vs-depth frontier for multi-controlled Toffoli/AND decompositions, including measurement-based uncompute variants that trade an ancilla qubit for lower T-depth. Practical guidance for the control-heavy gates in EC point-addition.

title: Improved T counts and active volume estimates for high-level arithmetic subroutines
abstract: Provides improved T-count and active-volume estimates for high-level arithmetic subroutines (adders, multipliers, modular reduction), analyzing how ancilla-borrowing and uncomputation choices affect both T-count and active volume under fault-tolerant cost models.
relevance_note: Connects ancilla/uncompute choices to active-volume cost for the exact arithmetic primitives used in EC/Shor circuits, helping pick space-saving constructions that also minimize the active-volume-dominated runtime cost.

title: Quantum resource estimates for computing binary elliptic curve discrete logarithms
abstract: Estimates logical and physical resources for binary ECDLP via Shor's algorithm, handling all exceptional cases of the point-addition law, and compares execution across quantum hardware architectures (photonic ~2-20x faster than baseline).
relevance_note: Binary-curve resource estimate that explicitly handles exceptional point-addition cases (point at infinity / equal points) — a correctness concern that affects qubit/ancilla budgeting in any reversible point-add circuit. Architecture comparison (photonic vs superconducting) informs which cost metric to optimize.

title: Resource analysis of Shor's elliptic curve algorithm with an improved quantum adder on a two-dimensional lattice
abstract: Proposes a carry-lookahead quantum adder whose Toffoli depth matches state-of-the-art while keeping O(n) ancillas, combined with windowed scalar multiplication, Montgomery representation, and quantum lookup tables plus dynamic-circuit techniques, targeting P-256 cryptanalysis.
relevance_note: P-256 estimate at ~4300 logical qubits using a carry-lookahead adder (SOTA Toffoli depth with only O(n) ancillas) + windowed mult + Montgomery rep + quantum tables. The low-ancilla carry-lookahead adder and Montgomery-representation arithmetic are concrete subroutine swaps for reducing depth without blowing up the qubit budget.

title: Verifiable Quantum Advantage via Optimized DQI Circuits (the 'Dialog representation' paper)
authors: Tanuj Khattar, Noah Shutty, Craig Gidney, Adam Zalcman, Noureldin Yosri, Dmitri Maslov, Ryan Babbush, Stephen P. Jordan
abstract: Builds end-to-end reversible quantum circuits for Reed-Solomon syndrome decoding via the Extended Euclidean Algorithm, for Decoded Quantum Interferometry on the Optimal Polynomial Intersection problem. Introduces the 'Dialog' representation: a decomposition of a pair of inputs (numbers or polynomials) into a sequence of small invertible matrix multiplications that records the execution trace of a constant-time/division-free EEA, giving a complete recipe to recover the original inputs from the output. Provides implicit Bezout-coefficient access at low qubit overhead.
relevance_note: ALMOST CERTAINLY Schrottenloher's [14] 'dialog representation' reference. The Dialog = the recorded sequence of small (2x2) divstep/EEA transition matrices (the 'branch bits' / transition history), NOT the full r,s coefficient registers. By storing only this compact execution trace you reconstruct Bezout coefficients implicitly on demand instead of carrying wide r,s registers throughout — capturing the low gate cost of division-free EEA (safegcd/jumpdivsteps) WITHOUT its usual qubit blow-up. This is the cleanest articulation of 'record branch bits to avoid r,s registers' for reversible modular inversion. Polynomial-EEA variant matches Schrottenloher's polynomial-GCD context.

title: Constructing Large Controlled Nots
abstract: Reduces a multi-controlled NOT (C^n X) into a linear number of Toffoli gates using ancilla bits, with constant factors that depend on whether the borrowed wires are dirty (borrowed, restored) or burnable (clean/zeroed).
relevance_note: Decompose a C^n X down a ladder of borrowed ancilla into O(n) Toffolis instead of textbook O(n^2). Mechanisms: (1) single-ancilla split: 'toggle the ancilla bit ON when half of the controls are ON, then use a single control on the ancilla to play the role of that half', turning C^n into two C^(n/2). (2) Linear construction with n-2 borrowed bits accumulates control intersections down a chain of Toffolis. Costs: ~4n gates with n borrowed (dirty) bits, ~n gates with n burnable (clean) bits, ~16n gates with a single borrowed bit. Key lever for point-add: any big multi-control (e.g. is-register-zero, conditional select) becomes linear-Toffoli with ancilla you already have lying around.

title: Concrete quantum cryptanalysis of binary elliptic curves
abstract: Optimizes the full reversible-circuit stack for Shor-ECDLP on binary (GF(2^n)) elliptic curves, with the primary optimization target being the number of logical qubits. Covers fixed-base-point scalar multiplication and all subroutines.
relevance_note: BINARY-CURVE QUBIT MINIMIZER. Reduces qubits to 7n + ceil(log2 n) + 9 and Toffoli to 48 n^3 + 8 n^(log2(3)+1) + 352 n^2 log2(n) + 512 n^2 + O(n^(log2 3)) for double-and-add scalar multiplication. The 7n+O(log n) space layout for GF(2^n) arithmetic and Karatsuba-style (n^log2 3) multiplication are reusable design patterns even when the benchmark is a prime curve.

title: Halving the cost of quantum addition
abstract: Reduces the number of T gates for an n-bit adder from 8n+O(1) to 4n+O(1) via a 'temporary logical-AND' construction that uses four T gates to store the AND of two qubits into an ancilla and zero T gates to later erase it (measurement-based uncompute).
relevance_note: Measurement-based AND uncompute / temporary-logical-AND halves the T cost: a Toffoli-as-AND costs 4 T to compute and 0 T to uncompute (Clifford + classically-controlled fixup after X-basis measurement). The single most reused T-count reduction trick in modern Shor/ECDLP circuits.

title: Optimizing Quantum Space Using Spooky Pebble Games
abstract: Reversible Computation 2023 paper introducing a constraint-solver-based approach to find spooky-pebble-game strategies that minimize quantum space (pebbles) by spending classical space (spooks), with empirical evaluation on benchmark circuits.
relevance_note: Companion conference version: concretely demonstrates qubit reductions from converting reversible uncomputes into measurement-based 'spook' moves backed by classical memory. Shows the practical pebble-count savings on real circuits.

title: Catalytic Embeddings of Quantum Circuits
abstract: Introduces catalytic embeddings for circuit synthesis: rather than repeatedly approximating gates, approximations are relegated to preparing a fixed number of reusable resource states ('catalysts'). Because catalysts are prepared once and returned unchanged, catalytic embeddings always yield shorter circuits in the limit of increasing gate count and target precision.
relevance_note: Catalytic embedding: a fixed pool of reusable catalyst qubits replaces per-operation gate approximation, amortizing ancilla cost across many uses and returning the catalysts untouched -- a space-time lever for rotation-heavy subroutines.

title: Quantum Circuit Designs of Point Doubling for Binary Elliptic Curves
abstract: Designs the previously-overlooked point-doubling subroutine of Shor-ECDLP on binary curves and analyzes its integration with point addition for complete double-and-add scalar multiplication.
relevance_note: Fills the point-doubling gap (most papers only cost the addition step). Relevant for any double-and-add scalar-multiplication route where doubling ancilla/Toffoli cost must be budgeted separately from generic addition; useful for completing a binary-curve cost model.

title: New Space-Efficient Quantum Algorithm for Binary Elliptic Curves using the Optimized Division Algorithm
abstract: Optimizes the reversible division (inversion + multiply) step for binary-curve Shor-ECDLP using an optimized GF(2^n) division/extended-GCD algorithm, reducing qubit usage versus Banegas et al. while preserving reversibility of the polynomial-GCD loop.
relevance_note: Concrete optimization of the reversible polynomial-EEA division for binary curves: shows how to lay out the polynomial divstep loop with reduced ancilla by reusing space across the shrinking-degree operands (binary-field analogue of Luo's length-register trick). Good cross-check on how branch-bit / transition recording is realized for polynomial GCD specifically.

title: Yoked surface codes
abstract: Nearly triples logical qubits per physical qubit in the teraquop regime by concatenating surface codes into high-density parity-check codes ('yokes' measured along rows, optionally columns, via lattice surgery), assuming only nearest-neighbor square-grid connectivity at p=1e-3.
relevance_note: YOKED CODES: ~3x more logical qubits per physical qubit for idle/storage qubits. Reduces the physical-qubit cost of the wide registers in an EC point-add; one of the FT-layer levers behind the recent qubit-count reductions.

title: Asymptotically Efficient Quantum Karatsuba Multiplication
abstract: Improves the space complexity of Karatsuba multiplication on a quantum computer from O(n^1.427) to O(n) while keeping O(n^lg3) gate complexity, by ensuring recursive calls add their outputs directly into subsections of the output register, avoiding storage and uncomputation of intermediate results.
relevance_note: Subquadratic-gate Karatsuba multiplier at linear ancilla: recursive add-into-output avoids storing/uncomputing partial products. The technique for reducing Toffoli count of large multipliers below the schoolbook O(n^2) while staying space-frugal.

title: Verifying Measurement Based Uncomputation
abstract: Explains and verifies the measurement-based uncomputation primitive: instead of running an uncompute circuit, measure the ancilla in the X basis and apply a classically-controlled phase fixup conditioned on the outcome. Shows the algebra by which the measurement removes the qubit while pushing a correctable phase onto the controls.
relevance_note: The atomic measurement-based-uncompute move: X-basis measure to deallocate an ancilla with zero gates, paying only a classically-controlled Z fixup. The building block for the temporary logical-AND and for spooky pebbling.

title: How to factor 2048 bit RSA integers in 8 hours using 20 million noisy qubits
abstract: Combines Shor 1994, Griffiths-Niu 1996, Zalka 2006, Fowler 2012, Ekerå-Håstad 2017, Ekerå 2017/2018, Gidney-Fowler 2019 and Gidney 2019 to factor 2048-bit RSA with ~20 million noisy physical qubits in ~8 hours, at physical error rate ~1e-3 and surface-code cycle time 1us. Spacetime volume is ~100x less than comparable earlier estimates. Uses windowed/coset-representation modular exponentiation, oblivious carry runways, and a CCZ magic-state factory.
relevance_note: Canonical surface-code SPACETIME-VOLUME cost model: the objective is physical-qubits x wall-clock-time, reported in 'megaqubitdays'. Justifies minimizing Toffoli/CCZ count because each Toffoli consumes a magic state whose factory dominates both qubit footprint and time. Establishes that the real FT cost is logical-volume (qubits x cycles) not raw gate count, and that distillation factories set the conversion rate from Toffoli-count to spacetime.

title: Quartz: Superoptimization of Quantum Circuits
abstract: Automatically generates candidate circuit-rewrite rules for an arbitrary gate set, verifies them with an automated theorem prover, then applies them via cost-based backtracking search. Discovers optimizations beyond hand-written rule sets across multiple gate sets.
relevance_note: Automated rewrite-rule generation + verified superoptimization: discovers gate-count/T-count-reducing transformations a human might miss, generalizing template matching to any gate set. Tooling for finding new ECC-circuit simplifications.

title: Assessing requirements to scale to practical quantum advantage
abstract: Microsoft's full-stack resource-estimation framework. Abstracts the layers of the FT stack and estimates physical resources for three scaled applications, finding 1e5-1e6 physical qubits are needed for practical advantage. Identifies qubit size, speed, and controllability as the critical hardware parameters and provides closed-form formulas relating logical-qubit count, T/Toffoli count, code distance, and runtime.
relevance_note: Provides an explicit, tool-backed cost model: runtime ~ (logical-cycle count) x (cycle time) and physical-qubit count ~ (logical-qubit count + factory count) x 2d^2, with logical-cycle count driven by T/Toffoli depth. Defines the 'logical qubit-cycle' / spacetime accounting that makes qubit-count x logical-depth (a proxy for qubit x Toffoli volume) the natural scalar objective for comparing FT circuits.

title: Factoring using 2n+2 qubits with Toffoli based modular multiplication
abstract: Implements Shor's algorithm to factor n-bit integers using only 2n+2 qubits with a purely Toffoli-based (no QFT-addition) modular multiplication circuit. Circuit depth O(n^3) and gate count O(n^3 log n), matching the space/time of Takahashi while being Toffoli-based.
relevance_note: 2n+2-qubit Shor with a fully Toffoli-based (non-Fourier) modular multiplier built on CDKM/Takahashi adders, comparison-based modular reduction, and windowed/controlled adds. The fault-tolerant-friendly counterpart to Beauregard's phase-arithmetic, central to Toffoli-count minimization.

title: Low overhead quantum computation using lattice surgery
abstract: Shows lattice surgery reduces storage overhead by >4x and distillation overhead by ~5x relative to braided defects, enabling algorithms with 10^8 T gates using only 3.7e5 physical qubits at p~1e-3. Argues defects/braids should be deprecated in favor of lattice surgery, and gives explicit physical-qubit and T-gate budget tradeoffs.
relevance_note: Quantifies the FT overhead as a function of T-count and physical error rate, giving a concrete 'physical qubits needed for X T gates' law. Demonstrates that the dominant levers on spacetime volume are storage (idle logical qubits) and distillation throughput, both of which scale with T/Toffoli volume; supports T/Toffoli count as a primary cost driver while showing layout/routing also matters.

title: Efficient magic state factories with a catalyzed |CCZ> to 2|T> transformation
abstract: Presents a |CCZ> magic-state factory with footprint 12d x 6d producing one |CCZ> every ~5.5d surface-code cycles, plus a catalyzed |CCZ>->2|T> transformation that yields T-factories 25% smaller and 2x faster than Fowler et al. Generalizes the catalysis to arbitrary phase angles.
relevance_note: Gives the explicit qubit-footprint and cycle-time of a Toffoli/CCZ factory — i.e. exactly how many qubit-cycles one logical Toffoli costs (a 12d x 6d patch x ~5.5d cycles). This is the concrete conversion constant that turns a Toffoli count into surface-code spacetime volume, underpinning the Gidney-Ekerå RSA estimate and any qubit x Toffoli cost proxy.

title: Efficient Controlled Phase Gradients
abstract: A 'reverse control' trick to make a phase gradient controllable without extra workspace qubits, by controlling pre/post operations that invert one gradient so it sums with another.
relevance_note: Make a phase-gradient controllable with no extra workspace: apply a square-rooted reversed gradient controlled by a toggle, undo, then apply a forward gradient, with Z-rotation fixups on the control. 'When the control is satisfied, the first phase gradient is inverted so it adds together with the other gradient into the operation we want.' Keeps O(n) gates / O(n lg n) depth, zero ancilla -- useful for controlled rotations in modular arithmetic.

title: Stim: a fast stabilizer circuit simulator
abstract: Stim, a fast stabilizer-circuit simulator using an improved tableau representation: analyzes a distance-100 surface code (20k qubits, 8M gates, 1M measurements) in 15s and samples full shots at 1 kHz, improving deterministic-measurement complexity from quadratic to linear.
relevance_note: TOOLING: the standard simulator for validating FT/QEC constructions and Clifford parts of circuits. Not an arithmetic optimization itself, but the verification backbone for any surface-code costing of a point-add pipeline.

title: Even More Efficient Quantum Computations of Chemistry Through Tensor Hypercontraction
abstract: Uses tensor hypercontraction to block-encode quantum-chemistry Hamiltonians with O~(N) Toffoli complexity, the lowest shown for arbitrary-basis chemistry. Phase estimation needs O(lambda/eps) repetitions where lambda is the 1-norm of coefficients. Reports full Toffoli-count and logical-qubit resource estimates for realistic molecules (e.g. FeMoco).
relevance_note: Explicitly adopts TOFFOLI COMPLEXITY x repetition count as the end-to-end cost figure of merit for FT algorithms, and reports Toffoli-count alongside logical-qubit count as the headline resource numbers. Strong evidence that 'minimize total Toffoli count (then qubits)' is the community-standard objective for surface-code-era algorithms, the assumption behind a qubit x Toffoli proxy.

title: Graph-theoretic Simplification of Quantum Circuits with the ZX-calculus
abstract: Develops the local-complementation and pivoting rewrite strategy that removes interior Clifford spiders from a ZX graph (graph-like normal form), giving a deterministic simplification routine that reduces the number of nodes (and 2-qubit gates) while preserving semantics.
relevance_note: Local-complementation + pivoting to eliminate interior Clifford spiders: the foundational ZX simplification that downstream T-count reducers build on. Reduces spider/edge count and CNOT count; the substrate for phase-teleportation T-count cuts.

title: A Verified Optimizer for Quantum Circuits (VOQC)
abstract: A formally verified (Coq) quantum circuit optimizer implementing gate cancellation, rotation merging, and not-propagation passes proven to preserve semantics; competitive with Qiskit/t|ket> while guaranteeing correctness.
relevance_note: Formally verified rotation-merging and gate-cancellation passes: machine-checked T/gate reduction, so optimizations applied to ECC arithmetic are provably correct (no silent miscompilation of the circuit).

title: The Spooky Pebble Game
abstract: Introduces the 'spooky pebble game', an extension of the reversible pebble game that allows Hadamard-basis measurements during uncomputation. Measuring leaves temporary phase errors ('ghosts') that are fixed up later by classically-controlled phase corrections. This lets quantum space be reclaimed before an uncomputation begins instead of after it ends, dramatically reducing qubit counts (e.g. ~log L pebbles in ~L log L steps).
relevance_note: Measurement-based uncomputation frees ancilla early at the cost of classically-controlled phase fixups ('ghosts'). Replaces many ancilla qubits with cheaper classical control bits -- the core space-saving idea behind spooky pebbling. Coined the term.

title: Fast constant-time gcd computation and modular inversion (safegcd)
abstract: Introduces 'divstep': a single branchless transition on a (delta, f, g) state that subsumes the parity and comparison branches of binary GCD into arithmetic on delta. n divsteps compute gcd and Bezout coefficients in constant time. Proves divstep-count bounds (e.g. 741 divsteps suffice for 256-bit). Also gives the polynomial-input variant. Applications: Curve25519 inversion, NTRU keygen.
relevance_note: The single most important primitive for reversible inversion. divstep is constant-time AND branchless: the entire control decision is folded into the sign of delta and the low bit of g, so each step is a fixed small reversible transformation (a 2x2 transition matrix on (f,g) plus a delta update). This eliminates the data-dependent branching that forces r,s bookkeeping in Kaliski. The proven divstep bound gives the exact unroll depth; the 2x2 matrix structure is what enables jumpdivstep batching (see secp256k1).

title: Magic State Distillation: Not as Costly as You Think
abstract: Rewrites distillation circuits to separate error-detecting from non-error-detecting qubits, allowing most distillation qubits to be encoded at low code distance. This sharply reduces the space-time cost and qubit count of distillation; in extreme cases distilling a magic state can cost less than performing a logical Clifford on full-distance qubits. Provides explicit qubit/time costs for a range of 15-to-1 and higher-yield factories.
relevance_note: Quantifies the per-magic-state spacetime cost (in qubit-cycles / tile-cycles) of distillation factories, the conversion factor that turns a Toffoli/T count into surface-code volume. Argues distillation is NOT the dominant cost, weakening the case for treating raw T-count as the sole objective and reinforcing that idle logical-qubit storage and routing volume matter too.

title: Flexible layout of surface code computations using AutoCCZ states
abstract: Constructs a self-correcting 'AutoCCZ' state with embedded delayed-choice CZs for completing gate teleportations, enabling efficient surface-code spacetime layouts for both depth-limited (ripple-carry addition) and Clifford-limited (QROM read) circuits. Finds that circuit-level Toffoli parallelism (e.g. carry-lookahead vs ripple-carry) does not reduce runtime below ~5M physical qubits.
relevance_note: AutoCCZ + QROM layout: self-correcting CCZ teleportation and efficient spacetime layout for QROM reads — the lookup tables that windowed arithmetic depends on. Confirms ripple-carry (small, serial) beats carry-lookahead (wide, parallel) at realistic qubit budgets, guiding the adder choice in a width-constrained point-add.

title: A new quantum ripple-carry addition circuit
abstract: Presents a new linear-depth ripple-carry quantum addition circuit. Previous addition circuits required linearly many ancillary qubits; this new adder uses only a single ancillary qubit, with lower depth and fewer gates than previous ripple-carry adders.
relevance_note: The canonical 1-ancilla in-place ripple-carry adder (CDKM). Uses the MAJ/UMA (majority / un-majority-and-add) gate pattern to do n-bit addition with a single ancilla qubit and 2n Toffoli gates. Foundational space-optimal adder reused in essentially every Shor/ECDLP modular-arithmetic circuit.

title: A logarithmic-depth quantum carry-lookahead adder
abstract: Borrows the classical carry-lookahead idea to build a quantum carry-lookahead (QCLA) adder that adds two n-bit numbers in O(log n) depth using O(n) ancillary qubits. Provides in-place and out-of-place versions, plus mod 2^n and mod 2^n-1 variants.
relevance_note: Log-depth carry-lookahead adder (QCLA) trading O(n) ancilla for O(log n) depth instead of O(n). The depth/ancilla trade-off knob versus the CDKM ripple adder; its P/G (propagate/generate) tree structure is the basis for low-T-depth adders and modular reduction.

title: Quantum Addition Circuits and Unbounded Fan-Out
abstract: Constructs an O(n)-depth, O(n)-size quantum addition circuit for two n-bit numbers with no ancillary qubits; exact size 7n-6, smaller than any prior ancilla-free adder. Using it, builds O(d(n))-depth O(n)-size adders and explores unbounded fan-out for lower depth.
relevance_note: Zero-ancilla ripple adder (Takahashi-Tani-Kunihiro), exact size 7n-6 with no ancilla qubits. The maximally space-frugal adder primitive; the qubit-count baseline for 2n+2 / 2n+3 Shor circuits where every saved ancilla matters.

title: Quantum Networks for Elementary Arithmetic Operations
abstract: Explicit quantum networks for basic arithmetic from addition through modular exponentiation. Identifies modular exponentiation as the most time- and space-consuming part of Shor's algorithm and analyzes the auxiliary memory required.
relevance_note: Original (VBE) reversible arithmetic circuits and the add-then-conditional-subtract modular adder template. The historical baseline (~7n-ish qubits, plain-Toffoli adders) against which all later qubit/Toffoli reductions are measured.

title: Quantum Carry-Save Arithmetic
abstract: Designs arithmetic elements from quantum gates using classical 'carry-save' techniques, enabling bit-parallel evaluation of all arithmetic needed for Shor's algorithm and deferring all carry propagation until the end of the computation, reducing depth.
relevance_note: Carry-save / redundant representation to defer carry propagation, cutting addition depth to O(1)-amortized inside multiply-accumulate. The technique behind constant-depth multiplier inner loops; a depth-vs-qubit lever for modular multiplication.

title: Circuit for Shor's algorithm using 2n+3 qubits
abstract: Minimizes qubit count for factoring an n-bit integer with Shor's algorithm: a circuit using 2n+3 qubits and O(n^3 lg n) elementary gates in O(n^3) depth, computable classically in polynomial time and fully general.
relevance_note: Defines the 2n+3-qubit Shor circuit via Draper Fourier-basis (phase) addition and the add-then-conditional-subtract modular adder, plus one-controlled-qubit-at-a-time semiclassical QFT. The reference design for extreme qubit minimization of the modular-exponentiation core.

title: Improved reversible and quantum circuits for Karatsuba-based integer multiplication
abstract: Presents a reversible Karatsuba multiplication circuit reducing space from O(n^1.585) to O(n^1.427) with less than a 2x increase in operation count and minor depth growth, with optimization insights derived from pebble games on complete ternary trees.
relevance_note: Reversible Karatsuba multiplier with pebble-game-driven ancilla management (space-time trade via recomputation). The earlier subquadratic multiplier that frames the qubit-vs-Toffoli trade-off Gidney later pushed to O(n) space; relevant to large modular multipliers in ECDLP.

title: Ancilla-Input and Garbage-Output Optimized Design of a Reversible Quantum Integer Multiplier
abstract: Proposes a garbage-free, ancilla-optimized reversible quantum integer multiplier using a novel add-and-rotate methodology that replaces shift-and-add with conditional adds plus rotate-right, producing zero garbage qubits and a 60-90% reduction in ancilla count over prior reversible multipliers.
relevance_note: Garbage-free, ancilla-minimized reversible multiplier (add-and-rotate). Representative of the dedicated reversible squaring/multiplier line (Jayashree-Thapliyal-Agrawal): a dedicated squarer reuses the operand on both inputs to roughly halve partial-product Toffolis vs a generic multiplier.

title: Synthesis and Optimization of Reversible Circuits - A Survey
abstract: Surveys reversible logic circuits and their applications. Categorizes work into synthesis, post-synthesis optimization, and technology mapping, reviewing search-based, cycle-based, transformation-based, and BDD-based synthesis (exact and heuristic), and identifies open challenges and misconceptions.
relevance_note: Reference survey of reversible-logic synthesis and post-synthesis optimization (gate-count/quantum-cost reduction, template/peephole rewriting, ancilla management). The methodological map for systematically reducing Toffoli/ancilla in arithmetic blocks.

title: Reversible Logic Circuit Synthesis
abstract: Foundational results on reversible circuits built from NOT/CNOT/Toffoli gates. Proves every even permutation is realizable without temporary storage (ancilla) using NOT, CNOT and TOFFOLI, gives optimal synthesis algorithms, and analyzes circuit sizes on three wires.
relevance_note: Establishes the NOT/CNOT/Toffoli universality and the ancilla-free realizability of even permutations, with optimal small-circuit synthesis. The theoretical underpinning for Toffoli-count lower bounds and template-based peephole optimization of reversible arithmetic.

title: Polynomial-Time T-Depth Optimization of Clifford+T Circuits Via Matroid Partitioning (T-par)
abstract: Models the Clifford+T circuit as a phase polynomial over parity terms and uses matroid partitioning to schedule T/T-dagger rotations in parallel layers, minimizing T-depth (and exposing T-count cancellations) in polynomial time. Basis of the 'T-par' optimizer.
relevance_note: Phase-polynomial + matroid partitioning ('T-par'): groups Rz(pi/4) rotations on identical parities so they cancel/merge, reducing both T-count and T-depth. Strong on arithmetic circuits like adders used in ECC.

title: T-count optimization and Reed-Muller codes
abstract: Shows the T-count minimization of a phase polynomial is equivalent to minimum-distance decoding of a punctured Reed-Muller code, giving optimal and heuristic (RM-based) algorithms that provably reduce T-count below earlier methods on many circuits.
relevance_note: Reed-Muller / coding-theoretic T-count minimization: reformulates T-count as RM(1,m) decoding, enabling provably better T-count for the phase-polynomial blocks (e.g. multiple-control gates, modular arithmetic).

title: Novel constructions for the fault-tolerant Toffoli gate
abstract: Gives a Toffoli realized with Clifford operations plus only four T gates (vs the conventional seven) using measurement and classical feedforward; adding n controls costs 4n T gates instead of 8n. Also provides an error-detecting variant.
relevance_note: Measurement-assisted Toffoli with 4 T instead of 7 (and 4n vs 8n for multi-controlled). Roughly halves the T-cost of every Toffoli; central lever for ECC point-addition circuits dominated by Toffoli/AND structure.

title: Halving the cost of quantum addition (measurement-based / temporary AND gates)
abstract: Introduces the measurement-uncomputed 'temporary logical-AND' gadget: an AND can be computed with 4 T gates and uncomputed with 0 T gates (Clifford + measurement + feedforward), and ripple-carry addition reuses a single ancilla, halving Toffoli/T cost relative to standard adders.
relevance_note: Gidney AND-gate: compute-AND = 4 T, uncompute-AND = 0 T via measurement. Halves the T-count of adders/comparators. The standard building block for cheap reversible modular arithmetic in ECC circuits.

title: On the advantages of using relative phase Toffolis with an application to multiple control Toffoli optimization
abstract: Shows that relative-phase (up-to-phase) Toffoli gates cost as few as 4 T gates and that multiple-control Toffoli gates can be built from relative-phase pieces whose spurious phases cancel between the compute and uncompute halves, cutting T-count and ancilla.
relevance_note: Relative-phase Toffoli: 4 T instead of 7 when a spurious controlled phase is later uncomputed. Composing MCT gates from relative-phase Toffolis (compute/uncompute cancel) sharply lowers T-count and removes ancilla.

title: Elementary gates for quantum computation
abstract: Foundational gate-count paper: upper/lower bounds for building 2- and 3-bit gates, asymptotic counts for n-bit Deutsch-Toffoli gates, and the canonical recursive decomposition of multi-controlled-U / multi-controlled-Toffoli into elementary gates (with and without ancilla).
relevance_note: Original multi-controlled-Toffoli decomposition (linear-with-ancilla and quadratic-without). Baseline against which all later MCT/T reductions (Maslov, Jones, He et al.) are measured; defines the ancilla/gate trade-offs.

title: Techniques for the Synthesis of Reversible Toffoli Networks (template/peephole matching)
abstract: Presents template (peephole) simplification of reversible Toffoli networks: a library of size-classified templates whose matched subsequences are replaced by shorter equivalents, plus a fast template-application algorithm and new templates of sizes 7 and 9.
relevance_note: Template / peephole matching: replaces locally matching gate runs with shorter equivalents and removes adjacent cancelling gates, reducing Toffoli/gate count in reversible logic. The classical complement to ZX-level optimization.

title: Time/Space Trade-Offs for Reversible Computation
abstract: Using a pebbling argument, shows that ordinary multitape Turing machines using time T and space S can be simulated by reversible ones using time O(T^(1+eps)) and space O(S log T), or in linear time and space O(S T^eps). Introduces a recursive, hierarchical scheme of doing and undoing various-sized portions of a computation -- the 'pebbling' algorithm viewed as a pebble game on a 1D chain of nodes.
relevance_note: Foundational reversible pebbling: trade recomputation time for fewer ancilla. The recursive do/undo strategy is the basis of every modern ancilla-recycling scheme; the O(S log T) vs O(S T^eps) curve is the canonical space-time knob for uncomputation.

title: A note on Bennett's time-space tradeoff for reversible computation
abstract: Refines and makes precise the time-space tradeoff of Bennett's reversible pebbling simulation, giving an exact analysis of the number of pebbles (registers) versus the number of computation/uncomputation steps for the chain pebble game.
relevance_note: Gives the exact pebble-count vs step-count formula for Bennett's chain game: with k+1 pebbles you can pebble 2^k nodes in 3^k steps. This is the precise lever for choosing how many ancilla registers to spend vs how much recomputation to pay.

title: Automatic synthesis of quantum circuits for point addition on ordinary binary elliptic curves
abstract: Software tool that auto-generates point-addition circuits for binary elliptic curves, achieving less than 39% of the T-gate count of the best previous construction without increasing qubits or T-depth, and optimizing CNOT depth via graph colorings.
relevance_note: EARLY AUTOMATED CIRCUIT SYNTHESIS. >60% T-gate reduction at equal qubit/T-depth, plus CNOT-depth minimization via graph coloring — an automated-search precedent for the island-hunting / synthesis approach. The graph-coloring CNOT-scheduling idea is reusable for depth optimization of any reversible arithmetic block.

title: The Montgomery Inverse and Its Applications
abstract: Introduces the Montgomery modular inverse: a right-shifting binary extended-Euclid algorithm that computes a^{-1}*2^k mod p (the 'almost inverse', Montgomery form). Splits into Phase I (binary EEA producing (r, k) with r = a^{-1}*2^k) and Phase II (k correction shifts). Uses only shift/add/subtract on (u,v,r,s) tuples, no division.
relevance_note: THE canonical (u,v,r,s) binary inverse that every quantum modular-inversion circuit (Roetteler, Haener, Luo) descends from. Phase I runs at most ~2n iterations, each a divstep-like branch on parity/comparison. In reversible form the four registers (u,v,r,s) plus the per-iteration branch bits are the dominant qubit cost — the whole optimization game (register sharing, branch-bit recording, length registers) is about shrinking exactly this.

title: Shor's discrete logarithm quantum algorithm for elliptic curves
abstract: First detailed implementation of Shor's discrete-log algorithm for elliptic curve groups over prime fields. Estimates ~1000 qubits to break 160-bit ECC vs ~2000 for equivalent 1024-bit RSA. Modular inversion (via extended GCD) is identified as the dominant cost of the affine group law, and the paper introduces the register-sharing idea to keep the inverse circuit space-efficient.
relevance_note: Origin of the 'inversion dominates point-addition space' framing and the register-sharing trick that later works refine. Their extended-GCD inverse reuses register space across the (u,v,r,s) evolution rather than allocating fresh registers per iteration; the modern descendants (Luo 2026, length registers + location-controlled arithmetic) are explicit reversible realizations of this idea. Note Proos-Zalka accepted some fidelity loss; recent work makes it exactly reversible.

title: A Game of Surface Codes: Large-Scale Quantum Computing with Lattice Surgery
abstract: Presents lattice-surgery surface-code schemes for small/intermediate/large scale, expressed as a tile-based 'game'. Encodes logical qubits as patches with low space cost and lays out explicit space-time trade-offs from slow/few-qubit to fast/many-qubit computations. Quantifies the cost of T/Toffoli gates and Clifford operations in terms of tiles (qubit patches) x time steps (code cycles).
relevance_note: Foundational tile-based cost model: a computation's cost is measured in TILES x CODE-CYCLES (logical spacetime volume). Shows a logical non-Clifford (T/Toffoli) gate costs a fixed number of tile-cycles to consume a magic state, and that the bottleneck is often the rate of magic-state consumption. Gives the precise vocabulary for converting 'qubit count' and 'Toffoli count' into a single spacetime-volume objective.

title: Surface codes: Towards practical large-scale quantum computation
abstract: The standard tutorial/reference on surface-code quantum computing: defines stabilizers on a 2D array, logical qubit construction, code distance d, and the relationship between physical error rate, distance, and logical error rate (p_L ~ (p/p_th)^(d/2)). Estimates the size and speed of a surface-code quantum computer and the physical-qubit overhead per logical qubit (~2d^2 physical qubits) and per code cycle.
relevance_note: Defines the base conversion from logical to physical resources: a logical qubit costs ~2d^2 physical qubits and one logical time-step costs ~d code cycles, so logical spacetime volume x ~d^3 = physical qubit-seconds. This is the foundational reason qubit-count and time both enter the cost as a product, and why higher Toffoli volume forces larger d or more factories, inflating physical spacetime.

title: Encoding Electronic Spectra in Quantum Circuits with Linear T Complexity
abstract: Constructs qubitization/QROM-based circuits that block-encode correlated-electron Hamiltonians with O(N + log(1/eps)) T-gate complexity. Introduces SELECT/PREPARE oracles and unary-iteration (QROM) techniques whose dominant cost is measured explicitly in Toffoli/T gates rather than arbitrary rotations.
relevance_note: Seminal example of using T-COUNT (and Toffoli count) as the primary algorithmic cost metric for FT algorithms, on the grounds that non-Clifford gates are the only ones requiring magic states and thus dominate surface-code spacetime. Motivates 'count the Toffolis/T' as the right first-order objective, and pioneered Toffoli-cheap constructions (unary iteration) that trade ancilla qubits for fewer non-Clifford gates.

title: A slightly smaller surface code S gate
abstract: A more space-efficient lattice-surgery construction for the logical S gate in the surface code.
relevance_note: Minor primitive: cheaper Clifford S in surface code; marginally reduces spacetime overhead of Clifford-heavy stretches. Low relevance to Toffoli/qubit count of the arithmetic itself.

title: Spooky Pebble Games and Irreversible Uncomputation
abstract: Extends reversible pebble games with measurement-based uncomputation: a pebble can be removed at any time, leaving a 50/50 'ghost' if dependencies are missing, deferring cleanup to reclaim space early.
relevance_note: Scheduling framework for measurement-uncomputation space/time tradeoffs. 'You can remove a pebble at any time; if a dependency is missing, there is a 50/50 chance the node will contain a ghost', and ghosts can be cleaned later. On a line this drops space from Omega(log n) to constant (S=3) and time from Theta(n^1.58) to Theta(n log n) via 'sweep and clean'. Lever: reclaim ancilla BEFORE the uncompute, letting deep point-add subroutines run in far fewer qubits.

title: Producing an N+1 Qubit CCZ State with an N Qubit Adder
abstract: Shows an n-qubit adder hides an (n+1)-qubit CCZ, proving an adder must consume at least N-1 CCZ magic states (a tight lower bound), and giving a recipe to extract a large CCZ state from an adder.
relevance_note: Adder <-> CCZ equivalence and magic-state lower bound. 'An N-qubit controlled increment decomposes into an uncontrolled N+1 increment and a bit flip; an N+1 increment decomposes into a triangle of C..CNOT gates; conjugate the largest C..CNOT by Hadamards and you have your N+1 qubit CCZ.' Establishes that an N-bit adder consumes >= N-1 three-qubit CCZ states -- a hard floor when budgeting Toffoli/CCZ for the additions in point-addition.

title: Constructing Large Increment Gates
abstract: Builds an n-bit increment (+1) gate from O(n) Toffoli-or-smaller gates using a single borrowed (dirty) ancilla bit, via a complement-and-subtract construction.
relevance_note: n-bit increment in O(n) Toffolis with ONE borrowed dirty ancilla instead of O(n^2). Mechanism: surround an increment with NOTs to turn it into a decrement ('increment the complemented value, then take the complement again ... turning it into a decrement'); use a borrowed register and two subtractions with toggled garbage bits that cancel, netting +1. Relevant because EC point-add is full of conditional +/-1 and counter updates; this gives the cheap linear increment primitive with negligible ancilla.

title: Turning Gradients into Additions into QFTs
abstract: Shows that adding into a pre-prepared phase-gradient register applies precise phase rotations, so QFT-style precise Z-gates can be replaced by ordinary additions, and the expensive precise gate need only be approximated once.
relevance_note: Replace many high-precision phase gates with additions into a phase-gradient register. Mechanism: 'when you shift samples in the time domain, you phase values in the frequency domain' and 'a phase gradient is unaffected when you add a into it ... except that a itself gets phased by a gradient.' Reduces more-precise-than-T phase gates from O(n lg n) to O(n) (prepare the gradient once, then reuse via cheap adders). Lever for any rotation/phasing inside arithmetic.

title: Proxy Phasing and Computed Phasing
abstract: Apply an arbitrary phasing function by computing membership/value into an ancilla, applying a Z (or phase gradient) to it, then uncomputing -- so a phase costs only the compute+uncompute of its function.
relevance_note: Compute-phase-uncompute: 'to phase a specific set of states, make a circuit that temporarily computes membership in that set and applies a Z gate to that computation's output'; 'any computable phasing function can be applied with the cost of merely computing and uncomputing that function.' For rotating n qubits by an angle, computing Hamming weight + phase gradient costs ~4n + m*lg(n) instead of m*n individually synthesized rotations. Cuts T-count for structured phasings.

title: Trouble Adding Constants into Qubit Registers
abstract: Notes that adding a compile-time constant is harder than register+register addition (no source register to use as workspace), and shows how to borrow workspace from the target register itself.
relevance_note: Constant-addition workspace trick: 'when adding a compile-time constant you don't have a source register to use as workspace.' Borrow an ancilla FROM the target register -- adding an even constant doesn't change the low bit's parity, so use that bit as workspace; for odd constants do a fixup increment first. Register-add is Theta(n) but best-known constant-add is O(n lg n). Relevant for adding curve constants (a, b, field prime) into registers without dedicated ancilla.

title: Simple Algorithm for Multiplicative Inverses mod 2^n
abstract: Computes K^-1 mod 2^n by reversing a multiplication circuit bit-by-bit; works in-place because high bits never carry into low bits under a power-of-2 modulus.
relevance_note: Invert multiplication-by-constant for free reversibly: 'the reverse of multiplying by K is multiplying by K^-1', and since 'high bits don't carry into low bits' mod 2^n, do conditional subtractions low-to-high in place with no workspace. Useful for Montgomery/2-adic inverse steps in modular arithmetic. Caveat: doesn't extend to non-power-of-2 moduli (circular dependency on the inverse).

title: Breaking Down the Quantum Swap
abstract: Decomposes a qubit SWAP into three CNOTs (XOR-swap) and generalizes to swapping arbitrary anticommuting observables, so data can be relocated without physically moving qubits.
relevance_note: SWAP = 3 CNOTs (XOR swap), and more generally any pair of anticommuting observables can store/move a qubit. 'Quantum mechanics is linear, so if it works for the basis states it works for every state.' Lets you relabel/relocate registers (e.g. fold a conditional swap into surrounding gates) instead of paying for physical moves -- cheap structural rewiring in point-add data routing.

title: Thinking of Operations as Controls
abstract: Argues operations and controls are interchangeable (only convention distinguishes them); reframing targets as X-axis controls yields more compact circuits and clarifies which qubit to treat as control vs target.
relevance_note: Control/target duality: 'X-axis controls condition operations on the control qubit being in the state H|1>'. Swapping which wire is control vs target can eliminate target designations and merge gates, shrinking circuits (demonstrated on Grover). A structural lever for re-expressing controlled arithmetic so controls fuse and fewer explicit operations remain.
